name: Increase Store Visits With Synup's Store Locator Pages
body_para:
Store locator pages are a must for any multi-location business. They make it simple for customers to find your business and access relevant information about the business location they plan to visit.

‍

With one click, customers can get driving directions on their navigation app of choice. That’s one more customer in the door, and one more sale.

‍

Synup makes it simple to create and build store locator pages that fit perfectly with your brand’s website. With mobile-compatible designs. drag-and-drop customization, and store info automatically populated from your Synup dashboard, you can easily increase store visits with Synup’s store locator pages.

‍

Why Do You Need a Store Locator Page?

Store locator pages facilitate frictionless conversions. Customers browsing your website will be able to easily find and navigate to the location closest to them, and you’ll be able to better track the customer journey.

‍

Our customers love implementing store locator pages to:

‍

  1. Improve the customer experience: store locator pages make it easy for customers to find the information they need with ease
  2. Boost local SEO: improve your visibility in local search by providing relevant details about your business locations
  3. Level up their online presence: multi-location businesses struggle to establish an online presence for all of their locations, but a store locator page does it all in one go
  4. Get better customer analytics: improve your understanding of the customer journey
  5. Improve brand consistency: a search on a third-party maps app will yield the same information, but the opportunities to brand and further inform your customers are priceless

‍

It’s Easy to Build a Store Locator Page With Synup

‍

We made it simple to create a store locator page right on the Synup platform. Each store page is search engine optimized with unique meta tags, schema, and content to ensure high ranking and discoverability on local search.

‍

Creating your page is simple. You can drag and drop elements that auto-populate based on your location data within Synup. The page is no-code and automatically formats for mobile viewing, too. Here’s what the creation process looks like:

‍

‍

Et voila, a store locator page is born.

‍

A Store Locator Page Drives Local Traffic

Store locator pages are an essential part of a multi-location business’ website. Once you build a store locator and implement local landing pages, you’ll see how you can drive traffic, improve local search rankings, and more.

‍

Happy building!

‍
use_case_summary: Store locator pages are essential for multi-location businesses. They make it easy for customers to find the location closest to them, get relevant information like store hours, then navigate to the store with ease.
